acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_dialogue_001] Pass if, in the revised banter around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:265, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:273, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:287, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:301, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:381, Silas and Damaris use contractions in most casual lines and can be told apart by rhythm before dialogue tags; Gideon at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:327 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:377 remains the only speaker consistently using conspicuous full forms. Fail if lines equivalent to 'You are asking the wrong bastard' or 'Do not look ill' remain without a clear dramatic reason.

[chapter_01_prose_001] Pass if the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:239, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:259, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:373, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:439 each arrive first through concrete action, sensation, or thought, and no more than two sentence-initial 'That was', 'That meant', or 'This was' pivots remain across those cited beats. Fail if those locations still read as labeled summaries instead of lived turns.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised banter around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:265, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:273, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:287, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:301, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:381, Silas and Damaris use contractions in most casual lines and can be told apart by rhythm before dialogue tags; Gideon at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:327 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:377 remains the only speaker consistently using conspicuous full forms. Fail if lines equivalent to 'You are asking the wrong bastard' or 'Do not look ill' remain without a clear dramatic reason.
findings.problem: In the Silas-Damaris material, two high-contraction speakers keep slipping into full-form, epigram-heavy diction. That smooths Silas and Damaris toward the same polished stage voice and weakens the contrast with Gideon's intentionally formal register from the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:265, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:273, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:287, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:301, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:313, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:355 so Silas sounds dirtier and more improvisational while Damaris sounds faster and more copy-sharp. Preserve Gideon's controlled low-contraction formality at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:327 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:377 as the deliberate exception.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:239, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:259, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:373, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:439 each arrive first through concrete action, sensation, or thought, and no more than two sentence-initial 'That was', 'That meant', or 'This was' pivots remain across those cited beats. Fail if those locations still read as labeled summaries instead of lived turns.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly lands turns with sentence-initial abstract pivots such as 'That was...' and 'That meant...'. Used this many times, the construction starts to read like scaffolding, flattening beats that should arrive through fresh image, bodily sensation, or immediate decision.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the pivot sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:239, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:259, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:373, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:423, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:439, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:445 so the turn lands through action, sensation, or pressure before any abstract naming of what the moment means.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
